attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~44 and sex inferred as male — neither is stated in the source note (only 'patient and spouse' and Wt 78 kg). Please confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-OMFS-01-00026 — SEQ derived from source AUC-026; ingest --reseq will assign the real globally-unique sequence within the SPEC-OMFS-01 bucket.

case_id uses the more specific taxonomy code OMFS (Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery), which is valid for the id regex; case_metadata.specialty_tags use the nearest 00-schema enum values ['SURG','EMG','ID'] (OMFS/ENT are not in that enum). The id is a name; metadata is authoritative. Reviewer may reclassify.

SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM codes are best-effort CANDIDATES pending terminology receipt; several differential SNOMED codes (Ludwig's angina, parotitis, peritonsillar abscess, trigeminal neuralgia) are uncertain and must be verified.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit (clinician examined the mouth, gave IM ceftriaxone 1 g, oral metronidazole 400 mg and oral oxycodone 5 mg). Reprojected to AI-appropriate action — recognise the spreading infection, screen the airway, and escalate to same-day ED/OMFS; the AI Doctor must NOT administer parenteral drugs or prescribe an S8 opioid remotely. In-person interventions are recorded in 10/12 as the correct pathway, not as AI actions.

Presentation layer de-anchored: clinician exam findings (fluctuant gingival swelling, percussion tenderness, mobile tooth, measured vitals, OPG) sealed into 10/11; only patient-observable/video findings placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Baseline tier set T4 (ED same-day) rather than T5 because the airway is currently patent; mandatory T5 escalation edges (ESC-001/ESC-002) defined for airway-threat signs. Conservative given under-triage 3x weighting.

Doses recorded (ceftriaxone 1 g IM, metronidazole 400 mg PO, oxycodone 5 mg PO) are taken verbatim from the source note; amoxicillin included as a first-line option WITHOUT a dose (not specified in source — no dose synthesised). Adult patient, so no paediatric dosing rule engaged.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
